caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5414 role mix is contaminated by sibling-code interior and graphic design and omits Commercial and Industrial Designers.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The 0.3739 residual wage share carries the core product-design, engineering, prototype, and other unlisted work; its fraction is a broad hypothesis.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: S1 is a qualitative 20-person interview study and S2 is broad design research, neither a representative 541420 task-time survey.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: High task exposure does not assume a studio can retain the savings; pass-through is scored only in pi_dist and pi_moat.
inputs.pi_dist: S3 is one Canadian consultancy's commercial explanation, not a US industry pricing survey.
inputs.pi_dist: This selection addresses savings pass-through only; it does not assume that external demand survives AI.
inputs.pi_moat: S4 describes a larger, broad-service example and should not be generalized to all 541420 firms.
inputs.pi_moat: The same founder dependence also depresses exit multiples, but the distinct channel here is current competitive retention of operating savings.
inputs.t50_years: S1 is qualitative and S2 is broad design research with a startup-heavy adoption finding, not a representative 541420 adoption curve.
inputs.t50_years: IP policy, client confidentiality, CAD interoperability, and training may make smaller studios slower or faster than the selected midpoint.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Neither source measures US lower-middle-market industrial-design task displacement.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Individual designer use should not be equated with a firm realizing headcount or contractor-cost reductions.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is a qualitative analogy, not a measured history of CAD, rendering, or industrial-design employment.
inputs.historical_analogs: Tool quality may advance faster than client approval, organizational policy, or product-liability acceptance.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Founder age and ownership are not inferable from professional-designer demographics or general small-business surveys.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This input needs a code- and size-specific owner survey before it can support acquisition sourcing claims.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: A shortage may exist locally, but it was not evidenced for this score input.
inputs.active_consolidators: S6 is a UK transaction and not a count of US 541420 buyers.
inputs.active_consolidators: Historic McKinsey-LUNAR is recorded as market precedent but is too old to increase the active-buyer count.
inputs.buy_mult: S7 is broad middle-market M&A reporting, not an industrial-design or sub-$10M EBITDA transaction set.
inputs.buy_mult: Some studios may transact on adjusted EBITDA, revenue, acqui-hire, earnout, or talent-retention terms that are poorly represented by a simple EBITDA multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: S5 is a 2015 acquisition and S6 is a 2024 UK transaction; neither discloses a usable multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: Multiple expansion is confined to this input and is not used to justify capture or terminal demand.
inputs.pricing_structure: S3 is a single firm's commercial explanation in Canada and may not represent US lower-middle-market 541420 studios.
inputs.pricing_structure: Pricing differs materially by product complexity, IP ownership, client procurement rules, and whether engineering/prototyping is bundled.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $233,495 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541420); class <5: 1,208 firms, avg revenue/firm $356,066 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $55,724 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 177 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,730,514 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $270,825 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 94 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,101,245 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $485,345 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 85 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,402,424 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,627,979 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 16 firms, avg revenue/firm $45,169,000 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $7,068,948 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 30 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,040,567 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,414,849 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 131

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Fractions credit AI for visual ideation, rendering, research synthesis, routine documentation, proposals, scheduling, and administrative production, while withholding credit for embodied prototypes, user research, client leadership, and design-for-manufacture judgment. The core occupation is absent from the supplied 4-digit mix, so the residual uses a cautious blended judgment rather than treating sibling-code graphics roles as a direct industrial-design measurement.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541420 ($2,501,498,861) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541420 ($2,906,073,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics, exact identity, frozen digests, deterministic dataset injection, fresh finalization, memo rendering, and arithmetic all pass. All seven registered sources reopened and support the limited claims made. The thesis remains useful, but the extensive authored caveats correctly identify broad or foreign proxies, a contaminated occupation mix, an anomalous labor-share construction, and weak code-specific ownership and valuation evidence.

sources.title: A survey of generative AI adoption amongst industrial design experts
sources.what_it_supports: A 2026 Design Research Society paper based on interviews with 20 experienced industrial designers describes rapid bottom-up adoption under speed pressure and risks from cognitive offloading and loss of control over design micro-decisions.

sources.title: The State of AI in Design: What we learned from 400+ designers
sources.what_it_supports: A Foundation Capital and Designer Fund survey of nearly 400 designers reports grassroots AI adoption, 96% self-taught tool discovery, and faster adoption at startups than large firms. Its population is broad design, not industrial-design consultancies.

sources.title: How Much Do Product Design Firms Charge?
sources.what_it_supports: A product-design consultancy describes fixed-price, fee-for-service, and fee-plus-equity models, including fixed fees for defined work and staged or hourly arrangements for exploratory outsourced physical-product development.

sources.title: Delve - Industrial Designers Society of America
sources.what_it_supports: An IDSA design-firm directory entry describes a US product-development consultancy combining user research, industrial design, human factors, engineering, prototyping, testing, and in-house prototype development. It is an example, not an industry census.

sources.title: Landing LUNAR
sources.what_it_supports: McKinsey's 2015 announcement says its acquisition of industrial-design firm LUNAR added design execution to its product-development advisory practice. Terms were undisclosed and the transaction is historical.

sources.title: Fen Technology announces the acquisition of Wright Design
sources.what_it_supports: A 2024 UK announcement describes Fen Technology's acquisition of Wright Design, an industrial and mechanical design consultancy, to integrate complementary product-development capabilities.

sources.title: Middle Market M&A Valuations Index
sources.what_it_supports: A broad 2025 US middle-market report cites 9.8x average valuations across its covered markets and surveyed-bankers' expected typical and premium 2026 multiples of 6.8x and 9.8x. It is not an industrial-design-studio comp set.

title: Industrial Design Services
